metadata.summary: Take control of your event routing.

documentation:
# Power-Events
[![Test](https://github.com/MLetrone/power-events/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://github.com/MLetrone/power-events/actions/workflows/ci.yml) [![Coverage](https://coverage-badge.samuelcolvin.workers.dev/MLetrone/power-events.svg)](https://coverage-badge.samuelcolvin.workers.dev/redirect/MLetrone/power-events) [![Language](https://img.shields.io/badge/Language-python≥3.9-3776ab?style=flat-square&logo=Python)](https://www.python.org/) ![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow?style=flat-square) ![Style](https://img.shields.io/badge/Style-ruff-9a9a9a?style=flat-square) ![Lint](https://img.shields.io/badge/Lint-ruff,%20mypy-brightgreen?style=flat-square) --- Source Code: [https://github.com/MLetrone/power-events](https://github.com/MLetrone/power-events) documentation: [https://mletrone.github.io/power-events](https://mletrone.github.io/power-events/) ---
_Take control of your event routing, effortlessly and tailored to your needs._ ## Description **Power-events** is a lightweight yet powerful library that manages event routing by defining routes with precise conditions, inspired by FastAPI’s endpoint routing system. It simplifies the organization of complex events by applying specific routing rules defined by the user and enables actions to be triggered in response to events in a structured manner. ## Key features: - **Syntax**: Lean to standards like Powertools or FastAPI. - **Easy**: Design to be easy to read and use. - **Flexible**: You can do whatever condition at any depth level in your event. - **Fully tested**: 100% coverage. - **Fully typed**: compatible out of the box with `mypy` ! - **Lightweight** ## installation ```shell pip install power-events ``` That's all! _Ready to use_ ## Examples --- ### Simple conditions - create a `main.py` with: ```python from typing import Any from power_events import EventResolver app = EventResolver() @app.equal("type", "order_created") def handler_order_created(event: dict[str, Any]) -> str: return f"Order created: {event['order_id']}" @app.one_of("type", ["order_update", "order_delete"]) def handle_order_modification(event: dict[str, Any]) -> str: return f"Order modification <{event['type']}>: {event['order_id']}" ``` - use routing: > N.B: assert is here to show which route has been called. > In real condition, don't need to use it. ```python assert app.resolve({ "type": "order_created", "order_id": "12345", "user_id": "67890" }) == ["Order created: 12345"] # Note: `resolve` returns a list, because you can allow multiple routes for one event. assert app.resolve({ "type": "order_delete", "order_id": "12345", "user_id": "67890" })== ["Order modification : 12345"] ``` ### Complex conditions _In depth field condition and condition combinations_ ```python from power_events import EventResolver from power_events.conditions import Value, Neg app = EventResolver() # Order created and digital purchase. @app.when(Value("type").equals("order_created") & Value("cart.is_digital").is_truthy()) def handle_digital_purchase(event: dict) -> str: return f"The order created is a digital purchase: {event['order_id']}" # Order created and physical. @app.when(Value("type").equals("order_created") & Neg(Value("cart.is_digital").is_truthy())) def handle_physical_purchase(event: dict) -> str: return f"The order created is a physical purchase: {event['order_id']}" assert app.resolve({ "type": "order_created", "order_id": "12345", "user_id": "67890", "cart": { "is_digital": True, "items": [ "$10 voucher" ] } }) == ["The order created is a digital purchase: 12345"] assert app.resolve({ "type": "order_created", "order_id": "12345", "user_id": "67890", "cart": { "is_digital": False, "items": [ "keyboard" ] } }) == ["The order created is a physical purchase: 12345"] ``` It's possible to perform `OR`, `AND`, `NEG` operation over conditions.

classes.name: power_events.exceptions.PowerEventsError
documentation: Base class for exceptions of power_events.

classes.name: power_events.exceptions.ValueAbsentError
documentation: Exception raised when the value to check is not present inside the event.

classes.name: power_events.exceptions.NoPredicateError

methods.name: power_events.exceptions.NoPredicateError.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the exception with the path that caused the error.

Args:
    path: The path of the value condition.
Exception raised when no predicate has been set for a value condition.

classes.name: power_events.exceptions.RouteError
documentation: Base class for exceptions related to routing errors.

classes.name: power_events.exceptions.NoRouteFoundError

methods.name: power_events.exceptions.NoRouteFoundError.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the exception with the event and the registered routes.

Args:
    event: The event that caused the error.
    routes: The list of registered route functions.
Exception raised when no route is found for an event.

classes.name: power_events.exceptions.MultipleRoutesError

methods.name: power_events.exceptions.MultipleRoutesError.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the exception with the event and the available routes.

Args:
    event: The event that caused the error.
    routes: The list of available route functions.
Exception raised when multiple routes are found for an event.

modules.name: power_events
documentation: Top-level Power event package.

modules.name: power_events.conditions
documentation:
Top-level conditions package.

This package contains all the logic about the condition.

functions.name: power_events.conditions.value.combine
documentation:
Combine all provided predicates into a single predicate.

Args:
    predicates: Predicates to combine.

classes.name: power_events.conditions.value.Absent
documentation: Sentinel class to represent value absence.

classes.name: power_events.conditions.value.ValuePath

methods.name: power_events.conditions.value.ValuePath.__new__
documentation:
Create a new ValuePath object from a path string.

Args:
    path: path string of a value inside a mapping object.
    separator: custom separator to use instead of the default.

methods.name: power_events.conditions.value.ValuePath.get_from
documentation:
Get the value describe by the path inside mapping object.

The value returns can be the sentinel `ABSENT` to differentiate real `None` value of default.

Args:
    mapping: Dictionary or mapping object to lookup.
    default: Default value if key not found. By default return the sentinel value `ABSENT`.
    raise_if_absent: Flag to raise `ValueAbsentError` if missing key. Default `False`

Note:
    Support both string and integer keys.

Raises:
    ValueAbsentError: if parameter `raise_if_absent` set, and key is missing.

methods.name: power_events.conditions.value.ValuePath.get
documentation:
Get the value describe by the path inside mapping object.

`get` is deprecated, use `get_from` instead.

The value returns can be the sentinel `ABSENT` to differentiate real `None` value of default.

Args:
    mapping: Dictionary or mapping object to lookup.
    default: Default value if key not found. By default return the sentinel value `ABSENT`.
    raise_if_absent: Flag to raise `ValueAbsentError` if missing key. Default `False`

Note:
    Support both string and integer keys.

Raises:
    ValueAbsentError: if parameter `raise_if_absent` set, and key is missing.

methods.name: power_events.conditions.value.ValuePath._validation
documentation: Validate string path.
A path-like string for accessing nested mappings value.

classes.name: power_events.conditions.value._MissingPredicate
documentation: A predicate that always returns False, representing a missing condition.

classes.name: power_events.conditions.value.Value

methods.name: power_events.conditions.value.Value.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the condition with the specified value path.

Args:
    value_path: The path to the value in the event.
    mapper: mapper to transform value before checking predicates on it.

methods.name: power_events.conditions.value.Value.root
documentation:
Initialize value targeting condition over the event itself.

Examples:
    ```python
    Value.root().contains("a").check(my_dict)
    #  is equivalent to.
    "a" in my_dict
    ```

methods.name: power_events.conditions.value.Value.check
documentation:
Check the given event respect the value condition.

By default, the event fails the check if the value is absent, an error can be raised,
by setting the flag `raise_if_absent`.

Raises:
    NoPredicateError: when no predicate has been set.
    ValueAbsentError: if a key define by the path is missing in event.

methods.name: power_events.conditions.value.Value.is_truthy
documentation: Add value is truthy check to the condition.

methods.name: power_events.conditions.value.Value.equals
documentation:
Add value equals the expected value check to the condition.

Args:
    expected: The expected value.

methods.name: power_events.conditions.value.Value.match_regex
documentation:
Add value matches the given regex pattern check to the condition.

Args:
    regex: regex to match (either a string or a Pattern)
    flags: regex flags; should bot be passed with a pattern.

Raises:
    TypeError: when passing flags whereas a `Pattern` have been passed

methods.name: power_events.conditions.value.Value.one_of
documentation:
Add value is one of the given options check to the condition.

Args:
    options: The container of options.

methods.name: power_events.conditions.value.Value.contains
documentation:
Add value contains all the given items to the condition.

Args:
    items: The items to check for.

methods.name: power_events.conditions.value.Value.is_not_empty
documentation: Add value is not empty to the condition.

methods.name: power_events.conditions.value.Value.is_length
documentation:
Add value has the specified size to the condition.

Args:
    length: The length expected.

methods.name: power_events.conditions.value.Value.match
documentation:
Add value matches the given predicate to the condition.

Args:
    predicate: The predicate to apply.

methods.name: power_events.conditions.value.Value.__add
documentation:
Add new predicate to the current one.

Args:
    predicate: The new predicate.
Condition based on a value at a certain path in an event.

classes.name: power_events.resolver.EventRoute

methods.name: power_events.resolver.EventRoute.match
documentation:
Check if the event matches the route's condition.

Args:
    event: The event to check.

methods.name: power_events.resolver.EventRoute.name
documentation: Get the name of the route function.
Class representing an event route with a condition and a function.

classes.name: power_events.resolver.EventResolver

methods.name: power_events.resolver.EventResolver.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the event resolver with optional configuration.

Args:
    allow_multiple_routes: option to allow multiples routes on same event, otherwise raise `MultipleRoutesError`.
    allow_no_route: option to allow no routes on event, otherwise raise `NoRouteFoundError`.

methods.name: power_events.resolver.EventResolver.equal
documentation:
Register a route with an equality condition.

Args:
    value_path: The path to the value in the event.
    expected: The expected value.

methods.name: power_events.resolver.EventResolver.one_of
documentation:
Register a route with a one-of condition.

Args:
    value_path: The path to the value in the event.
    options: The container of expected values.

methods.name: power_events.resolver.EventResolver.contain
documentation:
Register a route where value should contain items.

Args:
    value_path: The path to the value in the event.
    items: Items to in the event.

methods.name: power_events.resolver.EventResolver.when
documentation:
Register a route with a custom condition.

Args:
    condition: The condition to trigger this route.

methods.name: power_events.resolver.EventResolver.exception_handler
documentation:
Register a function to handle certain exception type.

Parameters:
    exc_type: exception type to handle.
        Could be either a unique exception type or a list of types.

methods.name: power_events.resolver.EventResolver.fallback
documentation: Register a fallback route if no registered routes match the event.

methods.name: power_events.resolver.EventResolver.resolve
documentation:
Resolve the event to the matching routes and execute their functions.

Args:
    event: The event to resolve.

methods.name: power_events.resolver.EventResolver._find_matching_routes
documentation: Find the routes matching the event.

methods.name: power_events.resolver.EventResolver._handle_not_found
documentation:
Handle cases where no routes match the event.

Args:
    event: The event to resolve.
    available_routes: The list of matching routes.

Raises:
    NoRouteFoundError: If no routes are found and not allowed.

methods.name: power_events.resolver.EventResolver._handle_multiple_routes
documentation:
Handle cases where multiple routes match the event.

Args:
    event: The event to resolve.
    available_routes: The list of matching routes.

Raises:
    MultipleRoutesError: If multiple routes are found and not allowed.

methods.name: power_events.resolver.EventResolver._lookup_exception_handler
documentation: Lookup the handler for the exception using Method Resolution Order, for matching against base exception.
Class responsible for resolving events against registered routes.

functions.name: power_events.conditions.condition.Neg
documentation:
Create a new condition representing the logical NOT of the given condition.

Args:
    condition: The condition to invert.

classes.name: power_events.conditions.condition.Condition

methods.name: power_events.conditions.condition.Condition.check
documentation:
Check if the condition holds for the given event.

Args:
    event: The event to check.

methods.name: power_events.conditions.condition.Condition.__or__
documentation:
Combine this condition with another condition using OR logic.

Args:
    other: The other condition.

methods.name: power_events.conditions.condition.Condition.__and__
documentation:
Combine this condition with another condition using AND logic.

Args:
    other: The other condition.

methods.name: power_events.conditions.condition.Condition.__invert__
documentation: Invert this condition (logical NOT).
Abstract base class for a condition that can be checked against an event.

classes.name: power_events.conditions.condition.ConditionOperator

methods.name: power_events.conditions.condition.ConditionOperator.__call__
documentation:
Apply the logical operator to the given conditions.

Args:
    condition: The conditions to combine.
Enum representing logical operators for condition combination.

classes.name: power_events.conditions.condition.ConditionExpression

methods.name: power_events.conditions.condition.ConditionExpression.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the composite condition with the specified conditions.

Args:
    conditions: The conditions that make up the expression.
Abstract base class for a composite condition expression.

classes.name: power_events.conditions.condition.Or
documentation: Composite condition representing the logical OR of its conditions.

classes.name: power_events.conditions.condition.And
documentation: Composite condition representing the logical AND of its conditions.
